```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width < PAD then PAD else x.width)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
    c.kind == x.kind &&
    c.value == x.value &&
    c.width >= PAD &&
    (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
    (x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD) &&
    Wellformed(c) &&
    Canonicalize(c) == c
{ }
```

**Explanation:**

**(a) Implementation:**
The function `Canonicalize` constructs a new `Id` with:
- The same `kind` and `value` (unchanged per spec)
- A `width` that is the maximum of the input width and `PAD` (4 digits minimum)

**(b) Specification ensures clauses:**
1. `c.kind == x.kind` — kind never changes
2. `c.value == x.value` — numeric value never changes
3. `c.width >= PAD` — result is at least 4 digits wide
4. `(x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width)` — ids already at 4+ digits are unchanged
5. `(x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD)` — ids below 4 digits are padded to 4
6. `Wellformed(c)` — the result is well-formed (width can hold the value)
7. `Canonicalize(c) == c` — canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op (idempotence)
